scenario_title: idempotent replay of a no-op delete inside one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified case where a delete_rr targets an RR that is definitely absent, while the UPDATE also contains a satisfied prerequisite and an unrelated add/delete pair. That shape lets servers either ignore the missing delete and return success or abort the message as NXRRSET-like failure. The follow-up QUERY on probe.campus.edu. provides a consistent post-state check, even if both implementations leave the zone unchanged, while the key divergence remains the UPDATE reply code.

group_1.behavior: Treat the absent delete_rr as a semantic no-op and apply the rest of the UPDATE.
group_1.post_state: probe.campus.edu. remains absent; the add/delete pair is committed and leaves no visible TXT rrset.

group_2.behavior: Treat the absent delete_rr as an error condition and reject the UPDATE.
group_2.post_state: probe.campus.edu. remains absent; no changes are committed.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.4 specifies delete_rr behavior when the target RR is present, but does not clearly define whether deleting an already-absent RR must be a benign no-op or an UPDATE-failing semantic error. With a satisfied prerequisite and unrelated operations in the same message, implementations may differ on whether to commit the message and what RCODE to return.
